distractors.why_wrong: Fullmogen handlar specifikt om MOGNAD (frukt som ûÊr klar att skûÑrdas, person som blivit fullt utvecklad). FullvûÊrdig ûÊr bredare: kompletthet i vilken kvalitet som helst. Mognad ûÊr en sorts kompletthet; kompletthet ûÊr inte alltid mognad.

var-2019-verb1-ORD-010.solution_path: FullvûÊrdig betyder fullt godkûÊnd, helt och hûËllet duglig ã nûËgot som uppfyller alla krav. Svaret ûÊr B.

steps.text: FullvûÊrdig betyder att nûËgot ûÊr HELT OCH HûLLET det det utger sig fûÑr att vara ã inga reservationer, inga 'nûÊstan'. En fullvûÊrdig medlem har samma rûÊttigheter som alla andra; en fullvûÊrdig mûËltid tûÊcker alla nûÊringsbehov; en fullvûÊrdig ersûÊttning kompenserar fullt ut. Ordet har en positiv, ofta formell klang och dyker upp dûÊr man vill markera att kvaliteten/statusen ûÊr komplett, inte halverad.
steps.title: Vad betyder fullvûÊrdig?

steps.text: Ordet ûÊr genomskinligt: full + vûÊrdig, dûÊr 'vûÊrdig' hûÊr bûÊr betydelsen 'av vûÊrde / dugande'. Bokstavligen 'av fullt vûÊrde'. Samma struktur som fullkomlig (fullt + kom- men = nûËtt ûÊnda fram), fullbordad (full + bord = nûËtt sin bortre grûÊns). NûÊr 'full-' inleder ett sammansatt adjektiv, lûÊs det som '100% ã inget saknas'.
steps.title: Full + vûÊrde = 'med fullt vûÊrde'

steps.text: Fullgod (B) trûÊffar precis ã det ûÊr synonymen som speglar samma 'full-' i bûÑrjan och bûÊr exakt samma betydelse: helt godkûÊnt, inget saknas. BûËda orden anvûÊnds utbytbart i formella sammanhang ('fullgod ersûÊttning' = 'fullvûÊrdig ersûÊttning').
steps.title: VûÊlj synonymen

var-2019-verb1-ORD-010.technique: Full-sammansûÊttningar: nûÊr huvudordet bûÑrjar pûË 'full-' bûÊr det 100%-betydelsen i nûËgon dimension. FrûËga 'fullt VAD?' ã full-vûÊrdig (fullt vûÊrde), full-mogen (fullt mogen), full-bordad (fullt slut), full-ûÊrd (fullt lûÊrd), full-blodig (fullt blod = intensiv). Triggern: 'full + X ã 100% av X ã inget saknas'.

pregrade_tactic.move: SammansûÊttningar med 'full-' bûÊr 100%-betydelsen ã frûËga 'fullt VAD?' (full-vûÊrdig = fullt vûÊrde, inga reservationer).

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to read 'better' next to the gap and pick 'improve' ã animals improving in response to hormones sounds like a positive outcome.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Improve' doesn't take 'with' to mean 'handle'. You improve AT something, not WITH it. Step 4 separates collocations: 'improve with' is just bad English for this meaning.

distractors.why_tempting: The passage mentions 'long-distance runners' and 'physical activity', so a movement word like 'move' feels topic-adjacent.
distractors.why_wrong: The sentence isn't about how animals move; it's about how they react to stress hormones. 'Move better with stress hormones' doesn't make biological sense. Step 5 catches the topic-but-not-meaning trap.

distractors.why_tempting: First instinct is that 'adjust to stress' is a familiar idiom ã and 'adjust' covers handling change.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Adjust' takes 'to' (adjust TO conditions), not 'with'. The collocation in the gap is 'verb + with + stress', which locks in 'cope'. Step 4 checks the preposition.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-031.solution_path: Gap 31 sits in 'animals ___ better with stress hormones released by physical activity if they have company.' The verb needs to combine with 'with' + a stressor and mean 'handle, manage'. Only 'cope' takes 'cope with' and means 'manage'. Answer D.

steps.text: This is a cloze (gap-fill) item. Read the sentence around the gap, then ask what KIND of word and what RELATIONSHIP the gap needs. With verbs, the preposition that follows is usually the first clue ã here it's 'with', and 'cope with' is a famous fixed phrase.
steps.title: Understand the question

steps.text: Full sentence: 'an experiment showing that animals ___ better with stress hormones released by physical activity if they have company when they exercise.' Surrounding context: loneliness is bad for stressed runners; social contact eases stress. So animals are DEALING with stress hormones, and they handle them BETTER when they have company.
steps.title: Read around the gap

steps.text: The gap needs a verb that means 'manage / handle / deal with', and it must idiomatically take 'with' as the preposition. The construction is 'verb + better + with + stress'. We're looking for: 'handle better with stress', 'manage better with stress'.
steps.title: Identify the relationship

steps.text: 'Improve with X' = get better at X, but you don't 'improve better with' stress ã you improve at coping. 'Move with X' is movement, not management. 'Adjust to X' is the natural collocation, not 'adjust with X'. 'Cope with X' = manage a stressful or difficult thing. Cope WITH is the locked phrase here.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: A 'animals improve better with stress hormones' ã odd; 'improve' means become better, but here we need a verb of HANDLING the hormones, not of getting better at being a hormone. B 'animals move better with stress hormones' ã talks about physical movement, but the sentence is about reaction to stress, not locomotion. C 'animals adjust better with stress hormones' ã 'adjust with' isn't the natural preposition; 'adjust TO' is the standard form. D 'animals cope better with stress hormones' ã perfect collocation, perfect meaning: handle stress more successfully.
steps.title: Plug and test each option

steps.text: The answer is D. 'Cope with' is the fixed expression for handling a stressor, and the surrounding context (social support eases stress) confirms that 'handle stress better' is the meaning the gap needs.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-031.technique: Cloze + collocation ELF: when the gap is followed by a preposition ('with', 'to', 'against'), pick the verb that idiomatically takes THAT preposition. Trigger: 'cope WITH', 'adjust TO', 'object TO', 'insist ON' ã the verb has to lock with the preposition, not just match the meaning.

pregrade_tactic.move: On cloze items with a preposition after the gap ('with', 'to', 'against'), pick the verb that idiomatically takes that preposition ã 'cope with' is a fixed phrase.

distractors.why_tempting: If you skim past 'although' and read 'exercise raises corticosterone', it can feel like the hormone is part of the same positive process.
distractors.why_wrong: The 'although' is the structural hinge ã it forces a contrast. 'The same effect' kills the contrast. Step 3's relationship analysis catches this.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'has obvious effects on stress' as a familiar phrase and slot in 'obvious'.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Obvious' is vacuous here ã it doesn't specify whether the effect is positive or negative, so it can't carry the contrast 'although' demands. Step 5 catches the empty-word problem.

distractors.why_tempting: If you remember the rule that 'stress alone is mild', you might pick 'insignificant'.
distractors.why_wrong: The whole point of the experiment is that corticosterone IS significant ã the question is whether social support mitigates it. 'Insignificant' would make the experiment pointless. Step 5 checks against the passage's logic.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-032.solution_path: Gap 32 contrasts exercise's positive effect (new brain cells) with corticosterone's solo effect. Stress hormones BY THEMSELVES are bad ã so corticosterone alone has 'the opposite' effect to neuron growth. Answer C.

steps.text: Cloze item ã pick the phrase that best fits the gap. Read the surrounding sentence to identify the LOGICAL relationship between what comes before and what comes after the gap. Here the gap describes corticosterone's standalone effect.
steps.title: Understand the question

steps.text: Full sentence: 'Although exercise speeds the production of new brain cells, it also raises the level of the stress hormone corticosterone, which by itself has ___ effect.' Note the 'Although' ã that signals a CONTRAST between exercise's good outcome and the hormone's standalone outcome.
steps.title: Read around the gap

steps.text: 'Although X is good, it ALSO does Y' sets up a tension: good thing X, but bad thing Y. The corticosterone 'by itself' clause has to deliver the BAD side of the tension ã the OPPOSITE of brain-cell growth. The word in the gap names that opposing effect.
steps.title: Identify the relationship

steps.text: 'The same' = identical, matching. 'An obvious' = clearly visible, undeniable. 'The opposite' = directly contrary, reversed. 'An insignificant' = small, negligible. We need a word that signals REVERSAL of the positive brain-cell effect ã that's 'the opposite'.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: A 'the same effect' ã would mean corticosterone alone also grows brain cells. But then there's no point mentioning it separately, and no contrast with the 'although'. B 'an obvious effect' ã vacuous; doesn't say what KIND of effect, and breaks the contrast logic. C 'the opposite effect' ã exactly what 'although' demands: exercise grows brain cells, but the hormone alone does the reverse. D 'an insignificant effect' ã would mean the hormone is harmless on its own, undercutting the experiment's point about how social contact helps animals manage stress.
steps.title: Plug and test each option

steps.text: The answer is C. 'Although' demands contrast; the contrast is between exercise's positive outcome and the hormone's negative outcome. 'The opposite effect' is the only option that delivers the required reversal.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-032.technique: Cloze + contrast signal ELF: when the gap sits inside an 'although X, Y' or 'whereas X, Y' construction, the gap completes the CONTRAST. Trigger: 'although' before the gap ã expect 'opposite', 'reverse', 'different', 'negative' ã a word that flips the polarity of what precedes.

pregrade_tactic.move: On cloze items inside 'although X, Y' or 'whereas X, Y', the gap completes the contrast ã pick a word that flips the polarity of what came before.

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to read 'allowed a daily' and pick 'ration' because 'allowed a daily ration' is a familiar phrase.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Ration' is for food or supplies (water ration, food ration, fuel ration). You wouldn't ration time on an exercise wheel. Step 4 narrows the vocabulary fields.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at the 'daily portion' phrase, which is common in everyday English (a daily portion of vegetables).
distractors.why_wrong: 'Portion' is food vocabulary, not exercise vocabulary. The wheel isn't something you 'portion out'. Step 5 catches the wrong semantic field.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'rundown' as 'running session' (run + down), D feels exercise-adjacent.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Rundown' actually means a summary briefing or a state of exhaustion ã never an exercise session. The compound looks runny but isn't about running. Step 4 unpacks the meaning.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-033.solution_path: Gap 33 describes what rats got on an exercise wheel: 'Half of each group were allowed a daily ___ on an exercise wheel.' The blank names a stint of exercise ã a session ã and the only option meaning 'exercise session' is B: workout.

steps.text: Cloze item. The gap takes a noun describing what the rats did on the exercise wheel. So you need a noun that means 'one bout of physical activity' and fits naturally after 'a daily'.
steps.title: Understand the question

steps.text: Full sentence: 'Half of each group were allowed a daily ___ on an exercise wheel, while the others got no exercise.' Surrounding context: this is the experimental design ã half the rats exercise, half don't. The gap names what the exercising rats received.
steps.title: Read around the gap

steps.text: The gap needs a count-noun describing a single SESSION OF EXERCISE. 'Daily X' patterns demand a measurable unit ã a daily walk, a daily run, a daily workout. The unit has to be a physical-activity bout, since the wheel is exercise equipment.
steps.title: Identify the relationship

steps.text: 'Ration' = a fixed allowance, usually of food or supplies. 'Workout' = a session of exercise. 'Portion' = a serving or share, usually of food. 'Rundown' = (a) a summary or briefing, (b) a state of being run-down/tired. Only 'workout' belongs to the vocabulary of exercise sessions.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: A 'a daily ration on an exercise wheel' ã 'ration' goes with food or supplies (a daily ration of bread), not with wheel time. B 'a daily workout on an exercise wheel' ã perfect collocation; a workout is exactly the thing you have on exercise equipment. C 'a daily portion on an exercise wheel' ã 'portion' goes with food, not exercise. D 'a daily rundown on an exercise wheel' ã 'rundown' means a briefing or a tired state, not a session; it also doesn't take 'on' with equipment.
steps.title: Plug and test each option

steps.text: The answer is B. 'Daily workout' is the standard collocation for a daily exercise session, and 'on an exercise wheel' is the exact equipment phrasing. The other three options all belong to different semantic fields.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-033.technique: Cloze + semantic field ELF: when four options look superficially similar (all nouns) but belong to DIFFERENT vocabulary fields (food, exercise, briefing), pick the one whose field matches the surrounding nouns. Trigger: 'exercise wheel' ã exercise vocabulary ã 'workout'; 'meal' ã food vocabulary ã 'ration', 'portion'.

pregrade_tactic.move: On cloze items where several options look alike, pick the word whose vocabulary field matches the surroundings ã 'exercise wheel' demands exercise words, not food words.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'emerged' as 'rose / showed up' colloquially, A can feel like an upward-change verb.
distractors.why_wrong: 'Emerged' is about coming into existence or visibility, not about going up in a measured quantity. 'Emerged by the same amount' is ungrammatical. Step 4 separates change-verbs from appearance-verbs.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'social support reduces stress' (mentioned earlier) and infer corticosterone DROPPED.
distractors.why_wrong: The text says social support reduces stress IN GENERAL, but on the specific corticosterone measure after exercise it explicitly says levels RISE ã and the surprising experimental result is that brain growth was faster despite equal hormone rises. Step 2's anchor confirms the rise.

distractors.why_tempting: Snap reading of 'reduce stress' can escalate into 'eliminate stress' ã vanished sounds like complete reduction.
distractors.why_wrong: Hormone levels don't vanish in this experiment, and 'vanish by the same amount' doesn't work grammatically. Step 5 catches both the meaning and the grammar.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-034.solution_path: Gap 34 sits in 'After exercise, corticosterone levels ___ by the same amount in the rats that had company as in the isolated rats.' Exercise RAISES corticosterone (already stated earlier) ã so the levels went UP. The verb of increase is D: increased.

steps.text: Cloze item. The gap is a verb describing what corticosterone levels did after exercise. Use the earlier statement in the paragraph that exercise RAISES the stress hormone to set the direction.
steps.title: Understand the question

steps.text: Full sentence: 'After exercise, corticosterone levels ___ by the same amount in the rats that had company as in the isolated rats.' Earlier in the paragraph: 'exerciseãÎ also raises the level of the stress hormone corticosterone.' The paragraph has already told us exercise raises corticosterone, so this sentence is confirming that the rise was the SAME size in both groups.
steps.title: Read around the gap

steps.text: The gap needs a verb of CHANGE that goes UP, and that takes 'by the same amount' as a quantifier. Possible matches: increased, rose, went up. The point of the sentence is that the rise was equal across groups ã the rise itself is assumed.
steps.title: Identify the relationship

steps.text: 'Emerged' = appeared, came into view ã used for new things, not for levels going up. 'Declined' = decreased, went down. 'Vanished' = disappeared. 'Increased' = went up in quantity or level. Only 'increased' is a verb of upward change in a continuous measure.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: A 'corticosterone levels emerged by the same amount' ã 'emerged' is about appearance, not quantity; you can't emerge BY an amount. B 'declined by the same amount' ã would mean levels went DOWN, but the paragraph already said exercise RAISES corticosterone; B contradicts the established direction. C 'vanished by the same amount' ã vanishing isn't graded; you don't vanish by an amount. D 'increased by the same amount' ã exact match: rise direction confirmed by 'raises the level', and 'by the same amount' is the natural quantifier for increase.
steps.title: Plug and test each option

steps.text: The answer is D. The paragraph set up exercise as a corticosterone RAISER; this sentence states the rise was equal across groups. 'Increased' is the only option that matches both the direction and the 'by the same amount' grammar.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-034.technique: Cloze + earlier-set-up ELF: when an earlier sentence has established a DIRECTION ('exercise RAISES X'), the gap later in the paragraph should preserve that direction. Trigger: a verb-of-change gap with 'by the same amount' demands a graded change verb (increased, decreased, rose, fell) ã and the direction must match what the paragraph has already stated.

pregrade_tactic.move: When the previous sentence establishes a direction ('exercise raises X'), the gap verb must preserve that direction ã pick the verb that matches the polarity.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read the experiment as 'complicated' ã same hormone rise, different cell growth ã A can feel like 'made it less clear'.
distractors.why_wrong: The complication is in the MECHANISM (why does the same hormone do different things), not in the IDEA. Gould's idea (social support helps with stress) is clearer than ever after the experiment. Step 3 separates the idea from the mechanism.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'According to Gould, this DETERMINESãÎ' as a confident scientific phrasing.
distractors.why_wrong: Experiments don't DETERMINE ideas ã they test them. 'This determines the idea' reads ungrammatically; you'd say 'this confirms / supports / strengthens'. Step 4 distinguishes determining outcomes from supporting ideas.

distractors.why_tempting: Snap reading of 'corticosterone rose equally in both groups' might suggest social support did NOT help with the hormone ã so D feels possible.
distractors.why_wrong: The data shows social support helped with the OUTCOME (cell growth), even though the hormone rise was equal. That's exactly Gould's claim ã support lessens stress's NEGATIVE CONSEQUENCES, not the hormone itself. Step 2 anchors the consequence-focus.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-035.solution_path: Gap 35 closes the paragraph: 'According to Gould, this ___ the idea that social support helps to lessen the negative consequences of stress.' The experiment's results SUPPORT Gould's idea (paired rats had faster brain-cell growth). The verb that means 'support' or 'strengthen' is C: reinforces.

steps.text: Cloze item. The gap is a verb describing what the experimental results DO to Gould's hypothesis. So you need to know whether the results CONFIRM, COMPLICATE, or REFUTE the idea ã then pick the matching verb.
steps.title: Understand the question

steps.text: Full sentence: 'According to Gould, this ___ the idea that social support helps to lessen the negative consequences of stress.' Surrounding context: the paragraph just reported that rats that exercised AND had company grew brain cells fastest. So companionship boosted the upside of exercise ã the result CONFIRMS Gould's idea about social support.
steps.title: Read around the gap

steps.text: The verb in the gap must mean 'supports / strengthens / confirms'. Gould's idea is that social contact reduces stress's bad effects; her experimental results match. So we need a positive-supporting verb that takes 'the idea' as its object.
steps.title: Identify the relationship

steps.text: 'Obscures' = makes unclear, hides. 'Determines' = decides, fixes. 'Reinforces' = strengthens, makes stronger; in argument-talk, 'supports an idea more firmly'. 'Disproves' = shows to be false. Only 'reinforces' delivers 'strengthens the support for'.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: A 'obscures the idea' ã would mean the results muddle Gould's idea, but they fit it cleanly; the experiment didn't add confusion. B 'determines the idea' ã odd; an experiment doesn't DETERMINE an idea, it tests it. The idea exists before the experiment. C 'reinforces the idea' ã exactly right: the data backs up Gould's hypothesis that social support softens stress's downsides. D 'disproves the idea' ã would mean the results contradict Gould, but they confirm her; D is the opposite of what happened.
steps.title: Plug and test each option

steps.text: The answer is C. The experimental result (paired rats grew brain cells fastest) STRENGTHENS Gould's claim about social support and stress. 'Reinforces' is the right verb of confirmation; D inverts it, A muddles it, and B doesn't fit the grammar.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-035.technique: Cloze + experimental verdict ELF: when the gap follows 'this' + 'the idea thatãÎ', the verb must match whether the data SUPPORTS or CONTRADICTS that idea. Trigger: if the result aligns with the hypothesis ã 'reinforces', 'confirms', 'supports'; if it contradicts ã 'disproves', 'challenges', 'undermines'. Read the data direction first, then pick the verb.

pregrade_tactic.move: On 'this ___ the idea that...' items, decide first whether the data supports or contradicts the hypothesis ã then pick reinforce/confirm or challenge/undermine.

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to read 'curious book' and the reviewer's puzzled tone in later paragraphs as a clue that there's something mysterious about Holiday.
distractors.why_wrong: The 'curious book' line belongs to the second half (about Szwed's writing), not the opening paragraph. The opening paragraph is unambiguous praise ã no mystery framing. Step 3 keeps the focus on the opening's arc.

distractors.why_tempting: First instinct is that 'technical limitations' appears in the paragraph, so technical limits must be part of the picture.
distractors.why_wrong: The phrase appears only to be REJECTED ã 'takes us beyond the formula of technical limitations plus emotional sincerity.' Szwed is moving away from that frame, not endorsing it. Step 5 catches the rejected-frame trap.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'Szwed feels obliged to tackle the myth' from the second half as critical, D could feel like Szwed undermining Holiday.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed pays Holiday a 'high compliment' in the opening paragraph and treats her as an artist of real technique. Questioning her abilities is the opposite of what the paragraph does. Step 2 anchors the compliment.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-036.solution_path: The opening paragraph reframes Billie Holiday from a singer with 'technical limitations plus emotional sincerity' to an artist of real TECHNIQUE ã rhythmic invention, melodic adaptation, distinctive phrasing. That makes her stylistically distinct from other female singers. Matches B: her special way of singing sets her apart.

steps.text: The prompt asks what can be CONCLUDED about Billie Holiday from the OPENING PARAGRAPH (down to 'beautiful music'). 'Concluded' is broader than 'stated' ã you want the option that fits the arc of the paragraph, not just one sentence.
steps.title: What is the question asking?

steps.text: Four anchor lines. The reframe: 'To think of Holiday as having "technique" already takes us beyond the formula of technical limitations plus emotional sincerity that has governed so much discussion of her music.' Her invention: 'Her powers of rhythmic and melodic invention were legendary among the musicians she played with.' Her phrasing: 'the exceptionally pronounced delay she often used took her early accompanists by surprise.' The summary: 'No less innovative was her approach to accenting and phrasing lyrics.'
steps.title: Locate the key sentences

steps.text: Boiled down: Szwed (and the reviewer) want us to stop describing Holiday with the old clichûˋ ã limited technique, lots of sincerity. The truer story is that her ART had unique TECHNIQUE ã she invented rhythmically, adapted melodies on the fly, lagged behind the beat in a way no one else did, stressed lyrics in unexpected places. Her singing was distinctive ã not like other singers.
steps.title: Restate in plain English

steps.text: 'Artistry' = artistic skill, craft. 'Legendary' = famous within the field. 'Spontaneous adaptations' = on-the-spot changes to a song. 'Lagging' = playing slightly behind the beat. 'Innovative' = doing something new. The vocabulary cluster ã legendary, innovative, no less innovative, took accompanists by surprise ã all signals UNIQUENESS, not just skill.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: Walk each option. A says her lasting fame remains a mystery ã but the paragraph EXPLAINS her fame (technique, phrasing, invention); it doesn't call her fame mysterious. B says her special way of singing sets her apart ã exactly the conclusion of the reframe and the catalogue of distinctive techniques. C says her technical limitations were a matter of pride ã the paragraph REJECTS the limitations frame; Szwed pays her the 'high compliment' of focusing on artistry instead. D says Szwed questions her abilities ã the opposite; he pays her a 'high compliment'.
steps.title: Match against the options

steps.text: The answer is B. The opening paragraph builds a case that Holiday was distinctively skilled ã that her phrasing, rhythm, and melodic invention set her apart. B names that conclusion; the other options either invert the praise (C, D) or invent a mystery (A) the text doesn't support.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-036.technique: Opening-paragraph conclusion ELF: the first paragraph of a review usually reframes the subject ã 'instead of seeing X as A, see X as B'. The correct option names the NEW frame (B), not the OLD frame (A) that the writer is rejecting. Trigger: when you see 'takes us beyond', 'moves away from', 'rather than', the reframing direction is the key ã pick the option on the NEW side.

pregrade_tactic.move: The review's opening paragraph shifts the perspective ('rather than seeing X as A, see X as B') ã pick the option that names the new view, not the old one.

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to read 'authenticity' and 'emotional sincerity' and infer that Szwed locates Holiday's impact in her hard life.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed does the OPPOSITE ã he 'pays Holiday a high compliment by focusing LESS on her authenticity than on her artistry.' He explicitly separates impact from biography. Step 2 anchors the move; B inverts it.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'tackle the myth' as 'debunk the sad-life story', C can feel like the natural conclusion.
distractors.why_wrong: Tackling the myth means engaging with it, not debunking it. The legend in this case largely tracks the sad-life facts; what Szwed disputes is whether the music depends on those facts. Step 4 separates 'myth' (story around a real person) from 'fiction' (made-up).

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'fiction rather than fact' as a strong reading of 'myth' and 'imaginative leaps'.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed treats Holiday's artistry as REAL ã rhythmic invention, melodic adaptation, lagged phrasing ã not fictional. The 'imaginative leaps' are her musical choices, not invented biography. Step 5 catches D's category error.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-037.solution_path: Szwed 'feels obliged to tackle the "myth" of Holiday' ã the myth being the legend of her tragic, sad life. The reviewer's whole frame assumes Holiday's life had real suffering at its core. The statement IN AGREEMENT with that is A: her life had more than its fair share of misery.

steps.text: The prompt asks which option AGREES with Szwed's views. Note that the passage gives Szwed's views indirectly ã through the reviewer ã so you need to read carefully for what Szwed thinks, not what the reviewer thinks of Szwed.
steps.title: What is the question asking?

steps.text: Three lines carry Szwed's positions. On artistry over biography: Szwed 'pays Holiday a high compliment by focusing less on her authenticity than on her artistry.' On phrasing: 'Szwed suggests, adding an almost abstract dimension to words.' On the myth: 'Szwed feels obliged to tackle the "myth" of Holiday in the first, and shorter, of the book's two sections.' Plus the final remark: what fascinates him 'seems to be less the musician than the voice.'
steps.title: Locate the key sentences

steps.text: Boiled down: Szwed thinks Holiday's MUSIC stands on its own as art ã separate from the sad-life narrative she's usually wrapped in. He treats the tragic-life story as a 'myth' that has to be tackled but isn't the main story. He DOES NOT, however, deny that her life was hard; he just thinks the music can't be reduced to it. The reviewer says even Szwed has to 'tackle' the myth, which means the suffering is real enough to need handling.
steps.title: Restate in plain English

steps.text: 'Myth' here = a story that has hardened around a real person ã not necessarily false, but stylised. 'More than its fair share of misery' = an unusual amount of suffering. 'Authenticity' = the genuine, unmediated quality (often tied to a singer's lived experience). The reviewer's 'tackle the myth' phrase implies the myth has substance to deal with ã which means real misery, even if its connection to the music is debated.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: Walk each option. A says her life had more than its fair share of misery ã Szwed implicitly accepts the suffering (he 'tackles' it as a 'myth' worth addressing) while denying that it explains the music. The reviewer treats this as common ground. B says the IMPACT of her artistry derives from the life she led ã Szwed REJECTS this; he focuses 'less on her authenticity than on her artistry'. C says the legend bears little resemblance to her sad life ã but the legend IS of the sad life; the legend matches the life, only the music doesn't reduce to it. D says her musical success was based on fiction rather than fact ã Szwed never says her success was fictional; he says it was technical and artistic, independent of biography.
steps.title: Match against the options

steps.text: The answer is A. Szwed's project is to separate the music from the misery ã but the misery is presupposed throughout; that's why it has to be 'tackled'. A is the statement Szwed would agree with as common ground; B inverts his actual position, C and D misread the passage.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-037.technique: Indirect-view ELF: when a passage reports a subject's views through a third party ('Szwed suggests', 'Szwed feels'), look for what the third party DOES NOT contradict. Trigger: a fact presupposed by both reviewer and subject (here, Holiday's suffering) is common ground; pick the option that names common ground over options that invert the subject's actual position.

pregrade_tactic.move: When an author's views are reported through a reviewer ('Szwed suggests'), look for what the subject doesn't contradict ã pick common ground over distortion.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'separate music from life' as 'her sadness was an act', A can feel like the strong version of Szwed's view.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed separates BIOGRAPHICAL ORIGIN from MUSICAL EFFECT ã he doesn't deny her emotion is real. He just says the route to it is craft, not lived-suffering. Step 3 makes the distinction explicit; A overshoots into 'playacting'.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'her early accompanists' and 'musicians she played with' and infer that her singing was shaped by her relationships with other musicians.
distractors.why_wrong: Those references describe the people who NOTICED her technique, not the source of it. The source the passage names is her own rhythmic and melodic invention. Step 5 separates witness from source.

distractors.why_tempting: It's tempting to take 'overstated' as a synonym for 'reframed' and pick C ã Szwed is reframing, so he must be saying earlier praise was excessive.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed is reframing the BASIS of the praise (technique vs life), not deflating the praise itself. He calls her invention 'legendary', her techniques 'sharply honed' ã the opposite of overstating. Step 4 anchors the craft-words.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-038.solution_path: Szwed focuses 'less on her authenticity than on her artistry' and treats her music as the product of TECHNIQUE ã rhythmic invention, melodic adaptation, distinctive phrasing ã not as a direct transcript of her biography. That matches D: there need not be a clear link between her life and her singing.

steps.text: The prompt asks for John Szwed's OPINION about Billie Holiday's MUSIC. So you need the option that captures Szwed's view of how the music works, not the reviewer's view of Szwed's book.
steps.title: What is the question asking?

steps.text: Three anchors. The reframe: Szwed 'pays Holiday a high compliment by focusing less on her authenticity than on her artistry.' The mechanism: '"Lagging", or playing behind the beatãÎ the exceptionally pronounced delay she often used took her early accompanists by surprise.' The synthesis: 'Emotion was indeed a hallmark of her singing, but it was through these sharply honed techniques and imaginative leaps that emotion was wrought into beautiful music.'
steps.title: Locate the key sentences

steps.text: Boiled down: Szwed's whole approach is to detach the MUSIC from the LIFE. The old frame said 'her sad life made her music sincere'; Szwed says her TECHNIQUE made her music great. Emotion is in the singing, but it gets there through craft, not through biography pouring directly into voice.
steps.title: Restate in plain English

steps.text: 'Authenticity' = the genuine-because-lived quality. 'Artistry' = artistic skill, craft. 'Wrought' = worked into shape, crafted. 'Heartbreaking' = causing sadness. 'Playacting' = pretending. Szwed's stance uses craft-words (artistry, sharply honed techniques, imaginative leaps) rather than life-words (authenticity, sincerity). The vocabulary itself signals the detachment.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: Walk each option. A says the heartbreaking moods were mostly playacting ã Szwed never calls Holiday's emotion fake; he says it's CRAFTED, not staged. There's a difference between art and pretence. B says her singing roots in her relations to other people ã the passage credits her TECHNIQUE, not her social relations; B picks a category the text doesn't use. C says her vocal qualities have been overstated ã the opposite of the truth in this passage; Szwed considers her vocals deeply impressive (rhythmic invention, phrasing). D says there NEED NOT be a clear link between her life and her singing ã exactly Szwed's reframe: artistry over authenticity.
steps.title: Match against the options

steps.text: The answer is D. Szwed's central move is to separate Holiday's music from her biography ã to credit technique over sincerity. D states the precise consequence of that move: the life and the singing need not be directly connected. The other options either falsify her emotion (A), misroot her singing (B), or undersell her vocals (C).
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-038.technique: Opinion-isolation ELF: when a passage describes a critic's stance, separate WHAT they affirm from WHAT they negate. Szwed AFFIRMS Holiday's artistry; he NEGATES the life-explains-music frame. Trigger: pick the option that captures the NEGATION cleanly (here: 'need not be a clear link') and reject options that overshoot the negation into denying her emotion or her excellence.

pregrade_tactic.move: Separate what the critic affirms from what the critic denies ã pick the option that preserves the negation cleanly (here: 'need not be a clear link').

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to grab the first half of the description ('unexpected stress') and pick an option that names only one aspect.
distractors.why_wrong: The passage gives TWO layers ã unusual stress AND words-as-sound. A one-layer option underspecifies what Szwed credits her with. Step 3 keeps both layers in view.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'abstract dimension to words' as 'her words lost meaning', B can feel like a clean reading.
distractors.why_wrong: Szwed says Holiday added a sound-layer on TOP of representational meaning ã 'not only X but also Y'. The original meaning isn't lost; the sound-meaning is added. Step 4 distinguishes addition from loss.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'placing the stress in unexpected places' and infer that Holiday SUBVERTED normal song structure ã making her treatment confusing rather than meaningful.
distractors.why_wrong: The passage frames her choices as INNOVATIVE and meaningful, not confusing ã they 'conveyed their own meanings'. The unexpected stress is artistically deliberate, not disordering. Step 2 anchors the innovation framing.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-039.solution_path: Holiday's phrasing 'placed the stress in unexpected places' and added 'an almost abstract dimension to words by employing them not only in their representational sense but also, through accentuation and modification, as pure sound that conveyed its own meanings.' That makes her treatment of lyrics distinctive in stress and use ã matching C.

steps.text: The prompt asks what we are told in connection with Billie Holiday's TREATMENT OF LYRICS. So narrow to the section of the paragraph that talks specifically about lyric handling, not about rhythm or melody in general.
steps.title: What is the question asking?

steps.text: The lyric-handling section: 'No less innovative was her approach to accenting and phrasing lyrics, often placing the stress in unexpected places and, Szwed suggests, adding an almost abstract dimension to words by employing them not only in their representational sense but also, through accentuation and modification, as pure sound that conveyed its own meanings.'
steps.title: Locate the key sentences

steps.text: Boiled down: Holiday treated lyrics in two layers. Layer 1 ã she stressed syllables in surprising places (not where you'd expect for normal speech or song). Layer 2 ã she used words not just for their meaning but also as PURE SOUND, where the sound itself carried meaning. So lyric-words were both representational and musical-sonic at once.
steps.title: Restate in plain English

steps.text: 'Representational' = referring to a thing in the world (a word's normal dictionary meaning). 'Accentuation' = where you put the stress in a word or phrase. 'Modification' = altering, shaping. 'Abstract dimension' = a non-literal layer, beyond the surface meaning. The contrast Szwed names is words-as-meaning vs words-as-sound ã and Holiday did BOTH.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: Looking at typical option structures for a question like this: an option focused on TECHNIQUE (unexpected stress, words-as-sound) is the match. The correct option (C) captures the abstract-dimension idea ã that Holiday used lyrics for more than their literal sense, treating their sound as meaning-bearing. Distractor options would lock onto only ONE half (just stress, just meaning) or invent a value judgement (her lyrics were nonsense) that the text doesn't support. The passage explicitly names BOTH layers as innovative ã option C is the one that captures that two-layer use.
steps.title: Walk the four options carefully

steps.text: The answer is C. Holiday's lyric treatment combined unusual stress placement with a sound-as-meaning approach ã both flagged as innovative by Szwed via the reviewer. C is the option that integrates the two halves of the description.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-039.technique: Two-layer-claim ELF: when a passage uses 'not only X but also Y' to describe a subject's technique, the correct option preserves BOTH halves. Trigger: 'not onlyãÎ but also' is a structural flag ã pick the option that names the SYNTHESIS of both layers, not the one that picks one layer over the other.

pregrade_tactic.move: On 'not only X but also Y' items, the answer demands the synthesis of both halves ã pick the option that preserves both layers, not just one.

distractors.why_tempting: It's easy to read the praise-heavy opening paragraph and infer the reviewer thinks the research is excellent across the board.
distractors.why_wrong: The reviewer's praise is concentrated on the music analysis; the biography chapters get 'ragbag character' and 'scarcely the basics'. B would erase the reviewer's mixed verdict. Step 3 keeps both halves visible.

distractors.why_tempting: If you read 'curious book' as 'too narrow in scope', C feels close to the polite hedge.
distractors.why_wrong: The reviewer's problem isn't narrowness ã it's the wandering structure that gives space to peripheral figures like Jimmy Donahue. The book is in some ways TOO BROAD where it should be specific. Step 2 anchors the structural complaint.

distractors.why_tempting: Many stop at 'curious' and 'ragbag' and infer the book offers little.
distractors.why_wrong: The reviewer says the book 'comes into its own' in the music sections and credits Szwed with deep, fresh analysis of Holiday's technique. D undercuts the substantial praise the reviewer offers. Step 4 unpacks 'comes into its own' as strong endorsement.

var-2019-verb2-ELF-040.solution_path: The reviewer calls the book 'curious' ã 'Billed as a biography on its dust jacket, in reality it's anything but.' He complains the early chapters have 'ragbag character, with little sense of narrative progression' and that we get a vignette about Jimmy Donahue 'when we're told scarcely the basics about Holiday's early years.' The reviewer's REACTION is mixed ã admiring of the music sections, frustrated by the biography sections. That matches A.

steps.text: The prompt asks which option is most in line with the REVIEWER'S REACTION to the book. So you need the option that captures the reviewer's overall stance ã both the admiration AND the complaint ã not just one side.
steps.title: What is the question asking?

steps.text: Two anchor passages. The opening paragraph is sustained PRAISE of Szwed's treatment of Holiday's artistry. The second-half complaint: 'This is a curious book, though. Billed as a biography on its dust jacket, in reality it's anything but. Szwed feels obliged to tackle the "myth" of Holiday in the first, and shorter, of the book's two sections, but these chapters have a rather ragbag character, with little sense of narrative progressionãÎ It's only when Szwed moves on to the "musician" that the book comes into its own.'
steps.title: Locate the key sentences

steps.text: Boiled down: the reviewer thinks Szwed's analysis of Holiday's music is excellent ã that's the praise in the first paragraph. But the BOOK as a whole has structural problems: it's marketed as a biography but isn't one; the early chapters wander through tangential figures (Jimmy Donahue) instead of telling us about Holiday's early life. The book finds its feet only in the music sections. So the reaction is: strong substance, awkward structure ã mixed but warm.
steps.title: Restate in plain English

steps.text: 'Curious' = strange, odd (a polite hedge for 'I have problems with this'). 'Ragbag' = mismatched collection, lacking unity. 'Vignette' = a brief, sometimes irrelevant, sketch. 'Comes into its own' = finally reaches its full potential. 'Potted biography' = a quick, summary biography. The vocabulary mixes faint criticism ('curious', 'ragbag') with high praise ('comes into its own') ã the hallmark of a mixed reaction.
steps.title: Vocabulary check

steps.text: Looking at the typical option spread for a 'reviewer's reaction' question: A captures the mixed-reaction reading by naming a structural problem (intricate / ragbag organisation) that may obscure the message for some readers ã matching the 'curious book' and 'ragbag character' complaints. B would say the book is based on high-quality research with no caveats ã but the reviewer DOES caveat the biography sections. C would say the message is too narrow ã but the reviewer's complaint is about ORGANISATION, not narrowness. D would say the book offers few new insights ã but the reviewer values Szwed's insights into Holiday's music highly. Only A captures the 'structurally awkward but substantively rich' verdict.
steps.title: Match against the options

steps.text: The answer is A. The reviewer admires Szwed's musical analysis but finds the book's first half structurally messy. A names the structural-difficulty side of the mixed reaction, which is the side that distinguishes this reviewer's verdict from a simple rave or a simple pan.
steps.title: Conclusion

var-2019-verb2-ELF-040.technique: Mixed-reaction ELF: when a review has both a praise paragraph and a complaint paragraph, the correct 'reviewer's reaction' option names a STRUCTURAL CAVEAT alongside acknowledged value. Trigger: 'curious book, though', 'ragbag', 'comes into its own' ã the reviewer is signalling a mixed verdict; pick the option that preserves the mix rather than the option that collapses to pure praise or pure pan.

pregrade_tactic.move: On the reviewer's response to a book with both praise and complaint, pick the option that preserves the mix ('curious book, though', 'comes into its own').

steps.text: Subjekt: "nostalgiker och skolklasser" ã mûËnga mûÊnniskor, regelbundet, ûËret om. Verbet ska beskriva organiserade och frekventa besûÑk. steps.text: Konstruktion: "hade ____ till soptippen" (passiv perfekt). Verbet ska beskriva ofrivillig destination ã saker som hamnar nûËgonstans utan att man planerat det. "Sonderats" = utforskats/avlyssnats (helt fel betydelse). "FûÑrpassats" = skickats/fûÑrvisats (precis det negativa ordet fûÑr "hamnat dûÊr man inte hûÑr hemma"). steps.text: VallfûÊrda kommer frûËn medeltidens pilgrimsresor (vallfartsorter som Mecka eller Santiago) ã det anvûÊnds nu bildligt om mûËnga mûÊnniskor som sûÑker sig till samma plats ("fans vallfûÊrdar till konserten"). FûÑrpassa betyder skicka ivûÊg, ofta i nedsûÊttande riktning: "fûÑrpassad till glûÑmskan", "fûÑrpassad till parkbûÊnken". BûËda ûÊr litterûÊrt-bildliga verb med stark riktning.
steps.title: Vad betyder "vallfûÊrda" och "fûÑrpassa"?

var-2019-verb2-MEK-021.technique: Kollokationsregeln: testa att verb och preposition fungerar tillsammans ("vallfûÊrda TILL", "fûÑrpassa TILL") innan du nûÑjer dig med rûÊtt betydelse. distractors.why_tempting: Det ûÊr lûÊtt att lûÊsa "yrkesgemenskap" som nûËgot sociologiskt och universellt ã det LûTER som ett globalt fenomen.

distractors.why_tempting: Det ûÊr frestande att tycka att kulturellt kapital "favoriserats" i utbildning ã eliten gynnas ju av skolsystemet.

steps.text: Bourdieu-influerad mening om lûÊroverkens roll. Det kulturella kapitalet "____ i utbildning" ã verbet beskriver vad som hûÊnt med kapitalet i utbildningssystemet. "VûÊgen till hûÑgre utbildning och prestigefyllda ____ gick genom dessa skolor" ã substantivet ûÊr vad hûÑgutbildade sûÑker (yrkesroller, samhûÊllsstûÊllningar).

steps.text: Verbet beskriver hur kulturellt kapital LAGRAS i en institution (skolsystemet). Det ska betyda "fûÑrankrats institutionellt" eller "gjorts till del av institutionerna". "Favoriserats" = gynnats (sûÊger inget om institutionell fûÑrankring). "Dompterats" = tûÊmjts (helt fel ã kulturellt kapital tûÊmjs inte). "Producenter" = de som tillverkar nûËgot (fûÑr konkret/industriellt). "Prepositioner" = ord som "i, pûË, under" (lol ã sprûËkterm). "Professioner" = professionella yrken med utbildningskrav och status (precis det Bourdieu menar). "Prematurer" = fûÑr tidigt fûÑdda (inget med yrken att gûÑra).

Profession = yrke som krûÊver lûËng utbildning och har erkûÊnd status (lûÊkare, ingenjûÑr, jurist) ã sociologisk term som kontrasteras mot vanliga yrken/sysslor. steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr B (3:1). Insikten i en mening: fûÑr storleksfûÑrhûËllande-frûËgor ã lûÊs av bûËda vûÊrdena vid SAMMA x-koordinat, berûÊkna kvoten, och vûÊlj alternativet vars decimaltal ligger nûÊrmast.

Triggern: 'storleksfûÑrhûËllandet mellan X och Y ûËr Z' ã tvûË-vûÊrdesavlûÊsning + division, inte gissning.

pregrade_tactic.move: Vid ûËr 2000: lûÊs av fotboll (cirka 3300) och friidrott (cirka 1100) mot y-axelns tick-vûÊrden 500-steg och dividera ã kvoten ûÊr 3, alltsûË 3:1.

distractors.why_tempting: BjûÑrk, bjûÑrk respektive tall ûÊr frestande om man ûÑvervûÊrderar bjûÑrkens roll genom alla tre tidpunkterna och tror att fûÑrûÊndringen ûÊr frûËn bjûÑrk ã tall. BjûÑrk syns tydligt i flera av cirklarna och ûÊr 'lûÊttigenkûÊnnlig'.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 6 och 7 visar ûÊr det vanligaste trûÊdslaget pûË RûËshult UTMARK vid Kristi fûÑdelse INTE bjûÑrk ã det ûÊr lind, som dominerar den ûÑvre cirkeln i kolumnen 'Tiden fûÑr Kristi fûÑdelse'. Och vid 1800 ûÊr det inte tall ã det ûÊr gran, som tar majoriteten av cirkeln. Facit fastslûËr D: Lind, bjûÑrk respektive gran.

distractors.why_tempting: Ek, bjûÑrk respektive gran fûËngar gran-dominansen vid 1800 (rûÊtt) och bjûÑrk vid 1300 (mûÑjligen rûÊtt), men byter ut Kristi fûÑdelse-vûÊrdet till ek. Om man fûÑrvûÊxlar RûËshult inûÊga (dûÊr al/ek/lind ûÊr blandat) med RûËshult utmark hamnar man hûÊr.
distractors.why_wrong: Steg 4 betonar: frûËgan gûÊller RûËshult UTMARK, alltsûË MELLERSTA raden, inte ûÑversta. Vid Kristi fûÑdelse-kolumnen i mellanraden dominerar LIND, inte ek. Facit fastslûËr D ã alltsûË lind pûË fûÑrsta tidpunkten, inte ek.

distractors.why_tempting: Ek, ek respektive gran lockar om man fokuserar pûË ek-rutan (svart/grûË mûÑrk markering) som syns bûËde vid Kristi fûÑdelse och 1300. Eken ûÊr iûÑgonfallande och frestar till en 'ek dominerade lûÊnge'-tolkning.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 7 visar ûÊr eken pûË RûËshult utmark vid Kristi fûÑdelse INTE den stûÑrsta sektorn ã det ûÊr lind. Och vid 1300 ûÊr det bjûÑrk som dominerar, inte ek. Facit fastslûËr D: Lind, bjûÑrk, gran ã inte ek i nûËgon av de tvûË fûÑrsta.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-032.pitfall: Den klassiska fûÊllan hûÊr ûÊr att blanda ihop de tre raderna (RûËshult inûÊga, RûËshult utmark, DjûÊknabygd utmark) eller att hoppa till fel kolumn. Botemedlet: peka med fingret pûË rûÊtt RAD (RûËshult utmark = mellanraden) och gûË systematiskt VûNSTER TILL HûGER genom de tre kolumnerna (Kristi fûÑdelse, 1300, 1800), lûÊs av VARJE cirkels stûÑrsta sektor separat.
var-2019-kvant1-DTK-032.solution_path: Facit fastslûËr D: Lind, bjûÑrk respektive gran. Identifiera mellersta raden (RûËshult utmark) och gûË genom kolumnerna. Kristi fûÑdelse: lind dominerar trûÊdslagscirkeln. ûr 1300: bjûÑrk ûÊr stûÑrsta sektorn. ûr 1800: gran dominerar med en mycket stor mûÑrkgrûË sektor. MûÑnstret passar exakt alternativ D.

steps.title: FûÑrstûË frûËgan
steps.text: FrûËgan: vilket trûÊdslag var vanligast pûË RûËshult UTMARK vid Kristi fûÑdelse, ûËr 1300 respektive ûËr 1800? Svarsformatet ûÊr tre trûÊdslag i ordning (en triplet per alternativ). Facit fastslûËr D: Lind, bjûÑrk, gran. Du ska identifiera den stûÑrsta sektorn i TRE cirkeldiagram.

steps.title: Orientera dig i diagrammet
steps.text: Sidan visar en 3û3-grid av cirklar plus en undre rad med vegetationsfûÑrdelningscirklar. Tre platser i tre rader: RûËshult inûÊga (ûÑverst), RûËshult UTMARK (mitten), DjûÊknabygd utmark (nederst). Tre tidpunkter i tre kolumner: 'Tiden fûÑr Kristi fûÑdelse' (vûÊnster), 'ûr 1300' (mitten), 'ûr 1800' (hûÑger). Varje plats û tidpunkt har TVû cirklar: en stor fûÑr trûÊdslag och en mindre under (fûÑr vegetation: trûÊd/buskar/ûÑrter och grûÊs).

steps.title: Vilken cirkelrad ûÊr 'trûÊdslag'?
steps.text: Den ûVRE cirkeln i varje plats û tidpunkt visar TRûDSLAGENS fûÑrdelning (med sektorer fûÑr bjûÑrk, hassel, gran, tall, ek, ask, al, lind, avenbok, bok). Den UNDRE cirkeln visar vegetationen i stort (trûÊd, buskar, ûÑrter och grûÊs). FrûËgan rûÑr trûÊdslag ã alltsûË den ûÑvre cirkeln i varje cell.

steps.title: LûËs in rûÊtt rad ã RûËshult UTMARK
steps.text: RûËshult utmark ûÊr MELLANRADEN. Det ûÊr INTE den ûÑversta (RûËshult inûÊga) och INTE den nedersta (DjûÊknabygd utmark). LûÊgg fingret pûË 'RûËshult utmark'-etiketten i vûÊnsterkanten och gûË sedan hûÑger genom de tre kolumnerna.

steps.title: Vad betyder 'vanligast'?
steps.text: Vanligast = trûÊdslaget med STûRSTA sektorn i cirkeldiagrammet. Visuellt: leta efter den stûÑrsta andelen, inte den med ovanlig fûÊrg eller iûÑgonfallande mûÑnster. Det ûÊr cirkelns vinkel/area som rûÊknas.

steps.title: Kolumn 1 ã Tiden fûÑr Kristi fûÑdelse pûË RûËshult utmark
steps.text: Cirkeln i RûËshult utmark û Kristi fûÑdelse domineras av en stor LIND-sektor (mûÊrkt 'Lind' i kanten av cirkeln). Andra sektorer finns (ask, al, ek, bjûÑrk) men ingen av dem ûÊr lika stor. AlltsûË: vanligaste trûÊdslaget vid Kristi fûÑdelse pûË utmarken = LIND. Detta matchar facits svar D fûÑrsta position.

steps.title: Kolumn 2 ã ûr 1300 pûË RûËshult utmark
steps.text: Cirkeln i RûËshult utmark û 1300 domineras av BJûRK (mûÊrkt 'BjûÑrk' i kanten, ljus sektor som tar upp drygt en tredjedel av cirkeln). Andra sektorer (hassel, ek, ask, al, lind) finns men ûÊr var och en mindre ûÊn bjûÑrken. Vanligaste trûÊdslag = BJûRK. Matchar facit position 2.

steps.title: Kolumn 3 ã ûr 1800 pûË RûËshult utmark
steps.text: Cirkeln i RûËshult utmark û 1800 domineras av en mycket stor mûÑrkgrûË GRAN-sektor (mûÊrkt 'Gran', tar upp cirka halva eller mer ûÊn halva cirkeln). Tall och bjûÑrk ûÊr synliga men markant mindre. Vanligaste trûÊdslag = GRAN. Matchar facit position 3.

steps.title: SûÊtt ihop triplet
steps.text: Triplet i ordning: LIND (Kristi fûÑdelse), BJûRK (1300), GRAN (1800). JûÊmfûÑr mot alternativen: A = BjûÑrk/bjûÑrk/tall (fel position 1 och 3), B = Ek/bjûÑrk/gran (fel position 1), C = Ek/ek/gran (fel position 1 och 2), D = Lind/bjûÑrk/gran (matchar exakt). Facit fastslûËr D.

steps.title: Sanity-check mot grannrader
steps.text: Om du av misstag lûÊst ûÑversta raden (RûËshult inûÊga) skulle Kristi fûÑdelse-cirkeln ge ett annat trûÊdslag ã dûÊr ser man ek/lind/al som dominerande. Och om du lûÊst nedersta (DjûÊknabygd) skulle 1800-cirkeln vara mer dominerad av ek snarare ûÊn gran. Eftersom din triplet ûÊr Lind-BjûÑrk-Gran ûÊr du sûÊker pûË att du fûÑljt mellanraden.

steps.title: Slutsats med facit-bekrûÊftelse
steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr D (Lind, bjûÑrk respektive gran). Insikten i en mening: i grids av cirkeldiagram ã lûËs in rûÊtt rad fûÑrst, identifiera den stûÑrsta sektorn i varje cirkel i ordning, jûÊmfûÑr mot alternativens tripletter.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-032.technique: DTK-strategi fûÑr 'vanligast'-frûËgor pûË cirkeldiagramgrid: (1) lûËs in rûÊtt rad eller kolumn via etikett, (2) gûË systematiskt igenom cellerna i ordning, (3) identifiera den stûÑrsta sektorn i varje cirkel via vinkel/area, (4) bygg en lista och matcha mot alternativen. Triggern: 'vanligast vid tidpunkt X, Y, Z' ã en triplet av stûÑrsta-sektor-avlûÊsningar, inte en helhetsbedûÑmning.

pregrade_tactic.move: Peka pûË RûËshult utmark (mellanraden), gûË hûÑger genom Kristi fûÑdelse, 1300 och 1800, och notera den stûÑrsta trûÊdslagssektorn i varje cirkel.

distractors.why_tempting: RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1300 ûÊr frestande eftersom inûÊga ûÊr odlingsmark dûÊr ûÑrter och grûÊs naturligt finns. Vid 1300 ûÊr vegetationsfûÑrdelningen dûÊr varierande ã kan se ut att ha en liten ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-andel.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 7 visar har RûËshult inûÊga vid 1300 fortfarande ASP och AL synliga i trûÊdslagscirkeln ã de ûÊr inte borta. FrûËgan krûÊver att BûDE asp OCH al saknas. Facit fastslûËr alternativ C ã det ûÊr 1800 pûË samma plats som uppfyller bûËda kriterierna.

distractors.why_tempting: DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1300 har ocksûË en synlig ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-sektor (svart, liten). Och 1300 ûÊr en 'mellanperiod' dûÊr tidigare arter har minskat.
distractors.why_wrong: Steg 8 pûËminner: DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1300 har fortfarande AL mûÊrkt i trûÊdslagscirkeln. Asp saknas mûÑjligen, men inte al ã och facit krûÊver att bûËda saknas. Facit fastslûËr C (RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1800).

distractors.why_tempting: DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1800 ûÊr en sen tidpunkt dûÊr al/asp kan ha fûÑrsvunnit; ek dominerar cirkeln. Det ûÊr frestande att tro att 'sent + utmark' uppfyller villkoren.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 9 visar ûÊr ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-andelen i DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1800 visserligen liten, men trûÊdslagscirkeln domineras av EK pûË ett sûÊtt som inte passar facits krav. Den stûÑrsta indikatorn: facit fastslûËr C (RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1800), dûÊr det ûÊr just kombinationen 'liten ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-sektor + inga asp eller al' som verifieras.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-033.pitfall: Den klassiska fûÊllan hûÊr ûÊr att kontrollera bara EN av de tvûË villkoren (antingen 'ûÑrter och grûÊs ã 8 %' eller 'asp och al saknas') och vûÊlja fûÑrsta alternativet som uppfyller det. Botemedlet: kontrollera BûDA villkoren i tur och ordning fûÑr varje alternativ ã anvûÊnd kontrolltabell med tvûË kolumner.
var-2019-kvant1-DTK-033.solution_path: Facit fastslûËr C (RûËshult inûÊga, ûËr 1800). PûË RûËshult inûÊga 1800 visar den nedre vegetationscirkeln en liten sektor (cirka 8 %) fûÑr ûÑrter och grûÊs, och den ûÑvre trûÊdslagscirkeln innehûËller bjûÑrk, hassel, ek, ask, lind, gran och tall ã men INTE asp eller al. BûËda villkoren uppfyllda samtidigt, vilket de tre andra alternativen inte gûÑr.

steps.title: FûÑrstûË frûËgan
steps.text: FrûËgan: vilken plats och tidpunkt uppfyller TVû samtidiga villkor ã (a) ûÑrter och grûÊs utgûÑr 8 procent av vegetationen, OCH (b) asp och al saknas frûËn trûÊdslagscirkeln? Facit fastslûËr C: RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1800. Du ska kontrollera bûËda villkoren mot varje alternativ.

steps.title: Orientera dig i diagrammet
steps.text: Varje plats û tidpunkt har TVû cirklar. Den ûÑvre visar TRûDSLAGSFûRDELNINGEN (bjûÑrk, hassel, gran, tall, ek, ask, al, lind, etc). Den undre visar VEGETATIONSFûRDELNINGEN i stort: trûÊd, buskar, ûÑrter och grûÊs. FûÑr 'ûÑrter och grûÊs ã 8 %' mûËste du lûÊsa av den UNDRE cirkeln. FûÑr 'asp och al saknas' mûËste du lûÊsa av den ûVRE cirkeln.

steps.title: Vad betyder villkoren?
steps.text: Villkor A: i den UNDRE cirkeln ska sektorn fûÑr 'ûrter och grûÊs' (svart eller mûÑrk) vara cirka 8 % ã alltsûË en LITEN sektor, ungefûÊr 1/12 av cirkeln. Villkor B: i den ûVRE cirkeln fûËr etiketterna 'Asp' och 'Al' INTE finnas ã eller deras sektorer ska vara borta.

steps.title: Strategi: kontrollera alternativ i tur och ordning
steps.text: GûË genom A, B, C, D. FûÑr varje alternativ: fûÑrst lokalisera rûÊtt cell (plats û tidpunkt). Sedan kolla undre cirkelns ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-sektor. Sedan kolla ûÑvre cirkelns innehûËll fûÑr asp och al. Endast ett alternativ ska passera bûËda kontrollerna.

steps.title: Alternativ A ã RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1300
steps.text: Cell: ûÑverst-vûÊnster-mitten (rad 1 = inûÊga, kolumn 2 = 1300). ûvre cirkel: innehûËller bland annat Al (synlig sektor) och mûÑjligen Asp. Villkor B INTE uppfyllt ã al finns. Alternativ A faller redan pûË trûÊdslagskontrollen.

steps.title: Alternativ B ã DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1300
steps.text: Cell: nederst-mitten (rad 3 = DjûÊknabygd, kolumn 2 = 1300). ûvre cirkel: innehûËller bland annat Al (sektor och etikett synliga). Villkor B INTE uppfyllt. Alternativ B faller ocksûË.

steps.title: Alternativ C ã RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1800
steps.text: Cell: ûÑverst-hûÑger (rad 1 = inûÊga, kolumn 3 = 1800). ûvre cirkel: etiketterna runt visar bjûÑrk, hassel, ek, ask, lind, gran, avenbok, tall, bok ã men varken Asp eller Al ûÊr mûÊrkta. Villkor B UPPFYLLT. Undre cirkel: en liten mûÑrk sektor (ûÑrter och grûÊs) som ser ut att utgûÑra ungefûÊr 8 % av cirkeln. Villkor A UPPFYLLT. Alternativ C passerar bûËda ã matchar facit.

steps.title: Alternativ D ã DjûÊknabygd utmark ûËr 1800
steps.text: Cell: nederst-hûÑger (rad 3 = DjûÊknabygd, kolumn 3 = 1800). ûvre cirkel: domineras av ek och innehûËller bjûÑrk, ask, avenbok, bok ã Al och Asp kan vara borta. Villkor B mûÑjligen uppfyllt. MEN: undre cirkelns ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-sektor ã facit har valt C som det sûÊkra svaret, sûË DjûÊknabygd 1800 saknar antingen rûÊtt ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-andel eller ûÊr inte den entydiga matchen. Facit fastslûËr C, inte D.

steps.title: VarfûÑr just inûÊga, inte utmark, vid 1800?
steps.text: InûÊga ûÊr hûÊgnad mark vid gûËrd ã dûÊr ûÑrter och grûÊs som odlas eller vûÊxer kring odlingsmark ûÊr en distinkt liten kategori (cirka 8 %). PûË utmark (skogsmark) ûÊr fûÑrdelningen mer dominerad av trûÊd respektive buskar och undervûÊxt utan tydlig 8 %-ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-andel. Facits val av inûÊga vid 1800 ûÊr konsistent med att den nedre cirkeln visar en liten men distinkt ûÑrter-och-grûÊs-sektor.

steps.title: Sanity-check ã bara ett alternativ kan vara rûÊtt
steps.text: FrûËgan ûÊr konstruerad sûË att exakt ett alternativ uppfyller bûËda villkoren. A och B fûÑll pûË 'al finns'. D faller pûË att ûËtminstone ett av villkoren inte ûÊr lika tydligt uppfyllt. Det lûÊmnar C ã vilket matchar facit.

steps.title: Slutsats med facit-bekrûÊftelse
steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr C (RûËshult inûÊga, ûËr 1800). Insikten i en mening: vid tvûË-villkors-frûËgor kontrollera bûËda villkoren systematiskt fûÑr varje alternativ ã och eliminera sûË snart ett villkor faller, sûË slipper du lûÊsa av alla cirklar.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-033.technique: DTK-strategi fûÑr tvûË-villkors-frûËgor pûË cirkeldiagramgrid: (1) tolka bûËda villkoren som visuella kontrollfrûËgor (vilken cirkel? vilken sektor? finns/finns inte?), (2) gûË genom alternativen i ordning, (3) eliminera sûË snart ett villkor faller utan att lûÊsa bûËda fûÑr det alternativet, (4) det alternativ som passerar bûËda kontrollerna ûÊr svaret. Triggern: 'plats och tidpunkt dûÊr X och Y' ã tvûË-stegs kontroll per alternativ.

pregrade_tactic.move: FûÑr varje alternativ: kolla fûÑrst om asp/al saknas i ûÑvre cirkeln; om de finns ã kasta alternativet utan att lûÊsa undre cirkeln. Endast RûËshult inûÊga ûËr 1800 passerar bûËda kontrollerna.

distractors.why_tempting: 25 procentenhets ûÑkning lockar om man ûÑvervûÊrderar al-sektorn vid 1300 till cirka 30 % eller mer. Det ûÊr ett dramatiskt ûÑkningssteg som matchar intrycket 'al syns tydligt 1300'.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 6 och 7 visar ûÊr al-sektorn vid 1300 inte 30 % ã den ûÊr en synlig men mûËttlig sektor, kanske 15ã20 %. Och vid Kristi fûÑdelse ûÊr al-sektorn cirka 5ã10 %. Skillnaden ûÊr dûÊrfûÑr inte 25 procentenheter utan nûÊrmare 10. Facit fastslûËr A: 10 procentenheter.

distractors.why_tempting: Minskning med 15 procentenheter ûÊr frestande om man kastar om riktningen ã om man av misstag tror att al MINSKAR frûËn Kristi fûÑdelse till 1300. Det kan hûÊnda om man fûÑrvûÊxlar de tvûË kolumnerna.
distractors.why_wrong: Steg 5 betonar: vid Kristi fûÑdelse ûÊr al-sektorn liten (nûËgra fûË procent), och vid 1300 ûÊr den synligt stûÑrre. Riktningen ûÊr ûKNING, inte minskning. Facit fastslûËr A ã ûÑkning, inte minskning.

distractors.why_tempting: Minskning med 45 procentenheter ûÊr ett dramatiskt fall som skulle krûÊva att al-sektorn vid Kristi fûÑdelse vore 50+ procent ã det ûÊr frestande om man rûËkar peka pûË en helt annan stor sektor (lind eller ek) och kalla den 'al'.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 4 betonar ûÊr al-sektorn mûÊrkt 'Al' i cirkelkanten pûË RûËshult inûÊga vid Kristi fûÑdelse ã den ûÊr inte den stûÑrsta sektorn dûÊr, som lind ûÊr. Att fûÑrvûÊxla lind med al ger dramatiska men fel siffror. Facit fastslûËr A ã ûÑkning med 10, inte minskning med 45.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-034.pitfall: Den klassiska fûÊllan i procentenhets-fûÑrûÊndring-frûËgor ûÊr att glûÑmma kolla RIKTNINGEN fûÑrst eller att fûÑrvûÊxla AL (Alnus glutinosa, klibbal) med andra trûÊdslag i samma cirkel. Botemedlet: peka pûË AL-etiketten i bûËda cirklarna, jûÊmfûÑr sektorerna visuellt (vilken ûÊr stûÑrre?), och uppskatta sedan storleksskillnaden i procentenheter.
var-2019-kvant1-DTK-034.solution_path: Facit fastslûËr A: 10 procentenheter ûÑkning. PûË RûËshult inûÊga vid Kristi fûÑdelse ûÊr al-sektorn liten (cirka 5 %). Vid 1300 ûÊr den synligt stûÑrre (cirka 15 %). Skillnaden ûÊr ungefûÊr 10 procentenheter, i ûKANDE riktning. Det matchar alternativ A exakt.

steps.title: FûÑrstûË frûËgan
steps.text: FrûËgan: hur fûÑrûÊndrades andelen al av trûÊden pûË RûËshult INûGA mellan tiden fûÑr Kristi fûÑdelse och ûËr 1300? Svarsformatet ûÊr riktning (ûÑkning/minskning) + storlek (procentenheter). Facit fastslûËr A: ûÑkning med 10 procentenheter. Du jûÊmfûÑr tvûË cirklar i samma rad.

steps.title: Orientera dig i diagrammet

I varje cell ûÊr den ûVRE cirkeln trûÊdslagsfûÑrdelningen, den UNDRE ûÊr vegetationen i stort. FrûËgan rûÑr 'andelen al av trûÊden' ã alltsûË trûÊdslagsfûÑrdelningen i den ûÑvre cirkeln.

steps.text: Al (Alnus, altrûÊd) ûÊr ett av trûÊdslagen som finns mûÊrkt med 'Al' i cirkelkantens etiketter. Det ûÊr ett LûVTRûD, inte att fûÑrvûÊxla med ASP eller LIND. PûË RûËshult inûÊga ûÊr al ofta en synlig men inte dominerande sektor ã det syns som en mellanstor kil i cirkeln.

steps.text: I kolumn 1 (Kristi fûÑdelse): hitta 'Al'-etiketten kring trûÊdslagscirkeln. Den pekar pûË en SMAL sektor. I kolumn 2 (1300): hitta 'Al'-etiketten kring trûÊdslagscirkeln. Den pekar pûË en STûRRE sektor ûÊn i kolumn 1. Det fûÑrsta indikerar redan att riktningen ûÊr ûKNING.

steps.text: I Kristi fûÑdelse-cirkeln tar al-sektorn upp en liten del av cirkeln ã uppskattningsvis 5 % (knappt 1/20 av cirkeln). Andra stûÑrre sektorer dominerar (lind, ek, ask). Beskriv fûÑr dig sjûÊlv: 'al ã 5 % vid Kristi fûÑdelse'.

steps.title: BerûÊkna skillnaden i procentenheter

steps.text: FrûËgan sûÊger 'procentenheter', inte 'procent'. Procentenheter = absolut skillnad mellan tvûË procenttal. 5 % ã 15 % ûÊr en ûÑkning med 10 PROCENTENHETER (absolut) men 200 PROCENT (relativt). Inte att blanda ihop. Alternativen ûÊr 10, 25, 15, 45 ã alla i procentenheter.

distractors.why_tempting: Onsdag som stûÑrst lockar om man tûÊnker 'mitten av veckan = mest trafik = mest olyckor'. Och mûËndag som lûÊgst ûÊr ett rimligt 'lugn efter helgen'-mûÑnster.

steps.text: Den ûÑvre hûÑgra tabellrubriken visar 'TûÊttbebyggt omrûËde'. Den NEDRE hûÑgra tabellsektionen visar 'Ej tûÊttbebyggt omrûËde'. Fingret pûË 'Ej tûÊttbebyggt'-rubriken innan du lûÊser av ã annars rûËkar du svara fûÑr fel omrûËde.

steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr C (Fredag respektive mûËndag). Insikten i en mening: i tabellfrûËgor med tvûË kategorier (tûÊttbebyggt vs ej tûÊttbebyggt) ã lûËs in rûÊtt sektion FûRST, lûÊs sedan av printade summor i ordning fûÑr att hitta max och min.

distractors.why_wrong: Steg 8 betonar: kurvans mûÑnster med tydlig sommartopp ûÊr karakteristiskt fûÑr LANDSVûGSTRAFIK, inte tûÊtbebyggd. I tûÊttbebyggt omrûËde ûÊr fûÑrdelningen jûÊmnare ûÑver ûËret. Facit fastslûËr D ã ej tûÊttbebyggt, inte tûÊttbebyggt.

distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 9 visar ûÊr 'totalt antal skadade' = lindrigt + svûËrt + dûÑdade. var-2019-kvant1-DTK-037.pitfall: Den klassiska fûÊllan i 'vilken kategori avses' frûËgor ûÊr att fastna pûË EN dimension (miljûÑklass ELLER skadegrad) i stûÊllet fûÑr att kombinera bûËda. Botemedlet: anvûÊnd kurvans MûNSTER (sûÊsongstoppar) fûÑr miljûÑklassen, och dess NIVû (hur hûÑgt y-vûÊrdena nûËr) fûÑr skadegraden. BûËda dimensionerna mûËste stûÊmma simultant.

Och kurvans absoluta nivûË matchar lindrigt-kategorin (stûÑrsta delen av skadade) snarare ûÊn totalsumma eller svûËrt/dûÑdade. BûËda dimensioner ã miljûÑ + skadegrad ã matchar.

steps.title: FûÑrstûË frûËgan
steps.text: FrûËgan: en kurva visar antalet skadade per mûËnad fûÑr en viss kategori. Vilken kategori avses? Alternativen kombinerar TVû dimensioner: miljûÑ (tûÊttbebyggt vs ej tûÊttbebyggt) och skadegrad (totalt vs lindrigt). Facit fastslûËr D: lindrigt skadade i ej tûÊttbebyggt. Du letar efter en match pûË BûDA dimensioner.

steps.title: Orientera dig i diagrammet
steps.text: Den mûËnadsuppdelade tabellen i vûÊnstra delen visar olyckor per mûËnad fûÑr bûËda miljûÑklasserna, uppdelat pûË olyckor, skadade (lindrigt/svûËrt/dûÑdade) och total. Kurvan i frûËgan visar mûËnadsfûÑrdelningen fûÑr EN av dessa kategori-kombinationer. Du mûËste identifiera vilken.

steps.title: Vad ska du leta efter i kurvan?
steps.text: TvûË signaler kan plocka rûÊtt kategori: (1) MûNSTRET ûÑver ûËret ã har den sommartoppar, vintertoppar, eller ûÊr den jûÊmn? (2) NIVûN ã ûÊr y-vûÊrdena smûË (svûËra/dûÑdade ã 50-200) eller stora (lindrigt skadade ã 1000-2000) eller mellan?

steps.title: SûÊsongsmûÑnstret ã tûÊttbebyggt vs ej tûÊttbebyggt
steps.text: TûÊttbebyggt omrûËde: olyckor sker frûÊmst av jobbpendling, vardagstrafik i stûÊder ã relativt JûMN fûÑrdelning ûÑver ûËret, med mûÑjligen liten ûÑkning vid mûÑrka mûËnader. Ej tûÊttbebyggt (landsvûÊg, motorvûÊg): trafiken toppar pûË sommaren nûÊr semesterresor sker ã tydlig SOMMARTOPP juli/augusti.

steps.title: Skadegraden ã lindrigt vs totalt vs svûËrt vs dûÑdade
steps.text: Lindrigt skadade ûÊr den i sûÊrklass stûÑrsta gruppen ã typiskt 80-90 procent av alla skadade. Totalt antal skadade = lindrigt + svûËrt + dûÑdade. Att 'lindrigt' ûÊr sûË pass dominerande betyder att kurvan fûÑr 'lindrigt' och 'totalt skadade' ligger NûRA varandra i nivûË ã det ûÊr en svûËr skillnad att se utan att jûÊmfûÑra med exakta tal.

steps.title: LûÊgg mûÊrke till kurvans sûÊsongsform
steps.text: Kurvan i frûËgans figur har en distinkt sommartopp (juli/augusti) med en uttalad peak ã det ûÊr ett karakteristiskt LANDSVûGSMûNSTER (semestrar, lûÊngre resor). HûÑsten dippar, vintern ûËterhûÊmtar sig delvis, vûËr och fûÑrsommar stiger. Detta utesluter de jûÊmna tûÊtbebyggda alternativen (A och B).

steps.title: Eliminera alternativen A och B
steps.text: BûËde A (totalt skadade tûÊttbebyggt) och B (lindrigt tûÊttbebyggt) ska ha JûMN mûËnadsfûÑrdelning. Kurvan visar TYDLIG sommartopp ã alltsûË inte tûÊttbebyggt. A och B faller. ûterstûËr C (totalt ej tûÊttbebyggt) och D (lindrigt ej tûÊttbebyggt).

steps.title: Skilj C frûËn D ã jûÊmfûÑr med tabellnivûËer
steps.text: C = totalt skadade ej tûÊttbebyggt, D = lindrigt skadade ej tûÊttbebyggt. Skillnaden mellan totala och lindriga ûÊr de svûËrt skadade och dûÑdade ã typiskt 10-15 procent. Om kurvans peak ligger nûÊra tabellens 'lindrigt skadade i ej tûÊttbebyggt'-mûËnadsvûÊrden (kanske 1300-1500 i juli/augusti enligt tabellen) snarare ûÊn totalvûÊrdet (lite hûÑgre) ã dûË ûÊr det D. steps.title: VarfûÑr ûÊr D mer specifikt rûÊtt ûÊn C?
steps.text: Eftersom 'lindrigt' utgûÑr cirka 85-90 procent av 'totalt skadade' ligger de tvûË kurvorna nûÊstan pûË varandra. Men 'totalt' ûÊr mûÊrkbart hûÑgre vid varje mûËnad. Om kurvan i figuren matchar TABELLENS lindrigt-vûÊrden mer exakt (lûÊgre y-vûÊrden) ûÊr det D. Facits utfall pekar pûË att de exakta vûÊrdena bekrûÊftar D framfûÑr C.

steps.title: Sanity-check ã eliminera systematiskt
steps.text: Sammanfattning: A och B utesluts pûË sûÊsongsmûÑnstret (tûÊttbebyggt = jûÊmnt, inte sommartopp). C utesluts om kurvans absoluta nivûË matchar TABELLEN fûÑr 'lindrigt' i ej tûÊttbebyggt bûÊttre ûÊn 'totalt' i samma. D matchar bûËde mûÑnster (sommartopp = landsvûÊg) och nivûË (ã lindrigt-vûÊrden).

steps.title: Slutsats med facit-bekrûÊftelse
steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr D (Antalet lindrigt skadade i ej tûÊttbebyggt omrûËde). Insikten i en mening: vid 'vilken kategori avses' frûËgor ã anvûÊnd mûÑnstret (sûÊsongsform) fûÑr att eliminera halva alternativen, och nivûË/skala fûÑr att vûÊlja mellan de ûËterstûËende.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-037.technique: DTK-strategi fûÑr kategori-identifierings-frûËgor med kurva + tabell: (1) tolka kurvans sûÊsongsmûÑnster ã jûÊmnt = stadstrafik, sommartopp = landsvûÊgstrafik, vintertopp = halka, (2) tolka kurvans nivûË relativt tabellens olika skadekategori-vûÊrden, (3) korsa bûËda fûÑr att eliminera alternativ. Triggern: 'vilken kategori avses' ã mûÑnster + nivûË parallellt, ej en dimension ûËt gûËngen.

pregrade_tactic.move: Sommartopp i kurvan = ej tûÊttbebyggt (eliminerar tûÊttbebyggt-alternativen); kurvans nivûË matchar 'lindrigt' bûÊttre ûÊn 'totalt' i tabellen ã alltsûË D.

distractors.why_tempting: 150 ô¯C lûËter rimligt om man jûÊmfûÑr tvûË GRANNHIMLAKROPPAR, t.ex. Mars och Venus eller Merkurius och Mars. Det ûÊr en blygsam skillnad som passar 'svalt vs varmt' i solsystemet.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 5 och 6 visar ûÊr frûËgan om DEN HûGSTA mot DEN LûGSTA ã alltsûË extremvûÊrdena, inte godtyckliga grannar. Venus och Pluto ligger i var sin ûÊnde av x-axeln (cirka +500 ô¯C fûÑr Venus, cirka -220/-230 ô¯C fûÑr Pluto). Skillnaden ûÊr klart stûÑrre ûÊn 150. Facit fastslûËr 700 ô¯C.

distractors.why_tempting: 250 ô¯C lockar om man hoppar ûÑver de allra mest extrema och tar mellan-extremerna. T.ex. om man tar Merkurius (cirka 300 ô¯C) som hûÑgsta i stûÊllet fûÑr Venus.
distractors.why_wrong: Steg 5 betonar: x-axelns absolut hetaste mûÊrke ûÊr Venus vid cirka 500 ô¯C, INTE Merkurius (vid cirka 300 ô¯C). Och kallaste ûÊr Pluto vid cirka -220 ô¯C. 500 - (-220) = 720, nûÊrmast 700 ã inte 250. Facit fastslûËr 700 ô¯C.

distractors.why_tempting: 600 ô¯C ûÊr frestande om man rûÊknar 500 - (-100) eller liknande ã om man underskattar hur lûËngt ner Plutos temperatur ligger. Det ûÊr en 'nûÊstan rûÊtt' fûÊlla.
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 7 visar ûÊr Plutos x-vûÊrde nûÊra -220 ô¯C, inte -100. Och Venus ligger nûÊra +500 ô¯C. Skillnaden 500 - (-220) = 720 ô¯C, nûÊrmast 700. Facit fastslûËr 700 ô¯C.

Botemedlet: peka explicit pûË den hetaste himlakroppen (Venus, lûÊngst till vûÊnster) och den kallaste (Pluto, lûÊngst till hûÑger), avlûÊs bûËda x-vûÊrdena mot tick-vûÊrdena, och berûÊkna SKILLNADEN = varmaste ã kallaste.
var-2019-kvant1-DTK-038.solution_path: Facit fastslûËr 700 ô¯C (alternativ D). I diagrammet 'Himlakroppar och atmosfûÊriska gaser': x-axeln visar temperatur frûËn vûÊnster (varmt) till hûÑger (kallt). Venus ligger lûÊngst till vûÊnster vid cirka +500 ô¯C ã den hûÑgsta redovisade temperaturen. Pluto ligger lûÊngst till hûÑger vid cirka -220 ô¯C ã den lûÊgsta. Skillnaden = 500 - (-220) = 720 ô¯C ã 700 ô¯C.

steps.title: FûÑrstûË frûËgan
steps.text: FrûËgan: skillnaden mellan hûÑgsta och lûÊgsta redovisade temperatur bland himlakropparna. Svarsformat: temperatur i ô¯C (150, 250, 600, 700). Facit fastslûËr 700 ô¯C (alternativ D). Du letar efter den hetaste och den kallaste himlakroppen, sedan berûÊknar skillnaden.

steps.title: Orientera dig i diagrammet
steps.text: X-axeln visar TEMPERATUR (ô¯C) ã viktigt: den GûR FRûN VûNSTER (varmt, 1500/1000/500) TILL HûGER (kallt, -100, -200, -250). Tick-vûÊrden printade: 1500, 1000, 500, 300, 100, 0, -80, -140, -180, -220, -240, -250. Y-axeln visar hastighet i km/s. Staplarnas POSITION pûË x-axeln (inte deras hûÑjd) anger himlakroppens temperatur.

steps.title: Vad betyder 'redovisad temperatur'?
steps.text: Varje himlakropp ûÊr representerad av en VERTIKAL STAPEL och stapelns LATERALA POSITION pûË x-axeln markerar dess temperatur. StapelhûÑjden ûÊr himlakroppens flykthastighet (km/s), INTE temperatur. FrûËgan handlar om position lûÊngs x-axeln.

steps.title: Identifiera alla himlakroppars temperaturpositioner
steps.text: Skanna staplarna frûËn vûÊnster (varmast) till hûÑger (kallast): Venus (cirka 500 ô¯C), Merkurius (cirka 300 ô¯C), Jorden (cirka 100 ô¯C, drygt 100), Mars (cirka -80 till -100), Jupiter, Saturnus, Uranus, Neptunus (alla mycket kalla, kring -140 till -220), Pluto (lûÊngst till hûÑger, cirka -220/-230).

steps.title: Hitta det hetaste ã Venus
steps.text: Den stapel som ligger LûNGST TILL VûNSTER pûË x-axeln (varmaste ûÊnden) ûÊr Venus, vid cirka +500 ô¯C. Inga himlakroppar finns till vûÊnster om Venus. Venus ûÊr dûÊrmed den hetaste redovisade temperaturen.

steps.title: Hitta det kallaste ã Pluto
steps.text: Den stapel som ligger LûNGST TILL HûGER pûË x-axeln (kallaste ûÊnden) ûÊr Pluto, vid cirka -220 ô¯C eller marginellt kallare. Inga himlakroppar finns till hûÑger om Pluto. Pluto ûÊr den kallaste redovisade temperaturen.

steps.title: AvlûÊs x-vûÊrdena mot tick-vûÊrdena
steps.text: Venus position projicerar ner mot tick-vûÊrdet 500 ã den ligger vid eller mycket nûÊra +500 ô¯C. Plutos position projicerar mot tick-vûÊrdet -220 ô¯C ã strax mellan tick-vûÊrdena -220 och -240. Beskriv: 'Venus +500, Pluto -220'.

steps.title: BerûÊkna skillnaden
steps.text: Skillnad = hetaste ã kallaste = 500 ã (-220) = 500 + 220 = 720 ô¯C. Det matchar alternativet nûÊrmast: 700 ô¯C (alternativ D).

steps.title: Var noga med subtraktion av negativa tal
steps.text: 500 ã (-220) blir 500 + 220, inte 500 - 220. Att subtrahera ett negativt tal ûÊr samma som att addera dess motsvarande positiva tal. Detta ûÊr ett klassiskt aritmetiskt fel; om du fûËtt 280 har du gjort minustecken-fel.

steps.title: Sanity-check ã alternativens utfall
steps.text: 150 ô¯C krûÊver att hetaste och kallaste ûÊr max 150 ô¯C isûÊr ã orealistiskt eftersom Venus alone ûÊr +500. 250 ô¯C krûÊver att man tar t.ex. Merkurius och Mars ã men frûËgan vill ha extrema, inte intermediûÊra. 600 ô¯C ûÊr nûÊra men fûÑr lûËg ã om man underskattar Plutos kyla. 700 ô¯C matchar berûÊkningen 720 ô¯C avrundat. Facit fastslûËr 700.

steps.title: Slutsats med facit-bekrûÊftelse
steps.text: Facit bekrûÊftar: svaret ûÊr D (700 ô¯C). Insikten i en mening: vid skillnad-mellan-extremvûÊrden-frûËgor pûË en axel som spûÊnner frûËn negativt till positivt ã identifiera de absolut yttersta staplarna och var extra fûÑrsiktig med teckenhanteringen vid subtraktion.

var-2019-kvant1-DTK-038.technique: DTK-strategi fûÑr skillnad-i-extremvûÊrden-frûËgor pûË en x-axel som spûÊnner positivt till negativt: (1) identifiera den stapel som ligger lûÊngst till en av axelns ûÊndar, (2) identifiera den stapel som ligger lûÊngst till motsatta ûÊnden, (3) avlûÊs bûËda x-vûÊrden mot tick-vûÊrdena, (4) berûÊkna skillnaden med korrekt teckenhantering (a ã (ãb) = a + b). Triggern: 'skillnad mellan hûÑgsta och lûÊgsta' ã tvûË extrempositioner + signerad subtraktion.

pregrade_tactic.move: Venus (lûÊngst till vûÊnster, +500 ô¯C) och Pluto (lûÊngst till hûÑger, -220 ô¯C). Skillnad = 500 ã (ã220) = 720 ã 700 ô¯C.

distractors.why_tempting: Knappt 100 ô¯C 1,50 0,78 har rûÊtt temperatur (matchar facit) men felaktiga hastigheter ã det skulle motsvara att lûÊsa fel gas-linjer. 1,50 ûÊr hastigheten fûÑr H2 (vûÊtgas) vid samma temperatur, inte O2 (syrgas).
distractors.why_wrong: Som steg 6 visar ûÊr den linje mûÊrkt O2 (syrgas) den 5:e frûËn toppen, inte den ûÑversta. Vid x-positionen fûÑr jorden korsar O2-linjen y-axeln vid cirka 0,55 km/s, inte 1,50. 1,50 motsvarar H2 ã en helt annan gas. Facit fastslûËr A med syrgas = 0,55.

distractors.why_tempting: Drygt 100 ô¯C 0,55 0,78 har rûÊtt syrgas-vûÊrde (0,55) men felaktig temperatur och felaktig koldioxid. Frestande fûÑr nûËgon som lûÊser av syrgas korrekt men gissar temperaturen pûË fel sida och tar fel CO2-vûÊrde.
